sections.paragraph:
and רְחֹוב f. ( Dan 9:25), plur. רְחֹבֹות (m., Zec 8:5).

(1) a street, so called from breadth; like the Gr. πλατεῖα, Gen 19:2 Jud. 19:20 .

(2) open place, forum, i.e. an ample space at the gate of Oriental cities, where trials were held, and wares set forth for sale, 2Ch 32:6 compare Neh 8:1, , 16 . Ezr 10:9, רְחֹוב בֵּית הָאֱלֹחִים “the open place before the house of God.”

(3) [Rehob], see רְחֹוב בֵּית.
